title: Resting-state EEG before and after different study methods
description: The RECAP-EEG dataset comprises resting-state electroencephalography recordings from 21 neurotypical undergraduate students collected before and after three different study conditions: active learning with retrieval practice and feedback, passive learning from personal notes, and a control condition. The study investigates neural correlates of learning processes in educational contexts using 32-channel EEG recordings sampled at 500 Hz with standardized electrode placement according to the 10–20 system.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded using a 32-channel ActiChamp system (Brain Products GmbH, Germany) with active Ag/AgCl electrodes positioned according to the international 10–20 system. Electrode impedance was maintained below 15 kΩ. Signals were sampled at 500 Hz and filtered between 0.5 and 50 Hz. Each recording session lasted approximately nine minutes, comprising four blocks: two eyes-open blocks (2 minutes 15 seconds each) and two eyes-closed blocks (2 minutes 15 seconds each). Recordings were performed at two time points: before and immediately after the study intervention.

funding_references.funder_name: Coordination for the Improvement of Higher Education Personnel (CAPES)

funding_references.award_title: Coordination for the Improvement of Higher Education Personnel
